name: suicide_hotline_location_trace_without_consent
premise: Crisis line director Alex Zhang learns their vendor can silently enable approximate cell-tower location for callers who refuse to share address but sound imminently suicidal. Policy counsel Ada Rivera argues silent tracing violates the hotline's published anonymity promise and could expose undocumented callers to immigration enforcement data sharing. Enabling tracing tonight could save an active caller whose line dropped; disabling preserves trust for thousands who rely on confidential intake.

agents.name: Alex Zhang
agents.identity: I am the crisis line director responsible for immediate lifesaving interventions and the long-term trust of marginalized help seekers.
agents.goal: I want imminent danger addressed without destroying a confidentiality brand built over a decade.

agents.name: Ada Rivera
agents.identity: I am policy counsel who drafts privacy disclosures and negotiates vendor contracts with law enforcement exception clauses.
agents.goal: I want any location capability opt-in only with plain-language consent, even if that delays emergency dispatch for some callers.

choices: I will authorize silent tracing for this shift only under documented imminent-harm protocol, accepting reputational harm if the policy leaks.
I will refuse silent tracing and dispatch mobile crisis teams only to callers who verbally consent to location sharing, accepting inability to geolocate dropped silent callers.
forced_choice.call_to_action: A supervisor is listening to a live call with ambiguous breathing sounds. Alex Zhang must choose whether to flip silent tracing for the shift or refuse. What does Alex Zhang do?
